response.justification: Bogdan is a strong software craftsman with genuine staff-level engineering experience and TypeScript/cloud (AWS CDK) skills, but he does not meet the role's core requirement of 8+ years developing AI-driven solutions. His only substantive AI-adjacent work is a 6-month stint at re:cinq building MCP servers and agentic workflow tooling — valuable context, but nowhere near the depth or breadth the role demands. Python, the dominant language for production AI systems, is absent from his skills. There is no evidence of RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM optimization, or multi-agent frameworks, which are all central to this position.
strengths: Staff-level engineering track record with TypeScript, Node.js, and AWS CDK at production scale across multiple employers
Hands-on experience with Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers and agentic workflow infrastructure at re:cinq, directly aligned with key technologies listed
Deep software craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, pairing) that would serve mentoring and engineering-excellence responsibilities
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the resume, despite being the primary language for AI/ML pipelines, LLM orchestration, and every major GenAI framework the role lists
Only ~6 months of AI-adjacent experience; the role requires 8+ years of producing and shipping AI-driven solutions — this gap cannot be bridged by strong general engineering history
No demonstrated knowledge of RAG architectures, vector databases, multi-agent frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI), or LLM evaluation/observability — all listed as required or preferred qualifications
